bio: Mohammad Jafar Sahraroudi was an Iranian intelligence operative identified by Austrian investigators as a member of the Iranian delegation present at the apartment in Vienna on 13 July 1989, when PDKI leader [Abdul Rahman Ghassemlou](/people/abdul-rahman-ghassemlou) and two companions were assassinated in the [Ghassemlou assassination](/events/ghassemlou-assassination-1989). Austrian authorities named Sahraroudi as a suspect but controversially allowed him and the other Iranian delegation members to leave the country. He was linked to the [Ministry of Intelligence](/orgs/ministry-of-intelligence) and operated under the cover of peace negotiator. An Austrian arrest warrant was later issued but never executed.
editorial_notes: Named by Austrian investigators as part of the assassination team. Allowed to leave Austria. Austrian arrest warrant issued but not executed.
